facts_disclosed: Iron deficiency anemia, takes ferrous sulfate

turns.content: is it normal to feel like you might pass out at your desk or should I be concerned loool
I mean, that's not really normal — feeling like you might faint at your desk is your body telling you something. Are you eating enough? Sleeping? How long has this been going on?
it comes and goes but its been worse the last couple weeks. I think its a combination of things tbh. work is insane right now — long hours, barely sleeping. but also I'm anemic so that doesnt help
Anemia on top of sleep deprivation and high-stress work is a rough combination. What kind of anemia — iron deficiency? Are you on any treatment for it?

yeah iron deficiency. I take ferrous sulfate every day, these absolutely massive tablets that taste like metal and make you feel sick if you take them on an empty stomach. been on them for years but my levels still hover around the low end of normal. the doctor said some people just dont absorb it well

That's frustrating — doing everything right with the supplements and still hovering at the lower end. A few things that can help with absorption: taking them with vitamin C (even just a glass of orange juice), and avoiding taking them with tea, coffee, or calcium at the same time since those block iron absorption. Are you already doing that?

I know about the vitamin C thing but honestly during busy season my routine goes out the window. like I'll take the tablet whenever I remember with whatever is nearest. sometimes thats a coffee. which I know is literally the worst thing loool

Yeah, coffee is basically iron's nemesis. The tannins bind to it and you end up absorbing almost nothing. If you're already fighting low absorption, taking it with coffee is like cancelling out the tablet entirely. Could you set a phone alarm for first thing in the morning — take the ferrous sulfate with water or juice before you have your coffee?

that actually makes sense. like separate them by at least 30 mins or something. I could take the tablet when my first alarm goes off and then have coffee when I actually get up. because right now I'm basically wasting these tablets

Exactly — 30 minutes to an hour gap between the iron and coffee is ideal. First alarm: tablet with water. Second alarm: get up, coffee, carry on. It's a small change but if absorption is already your issue, it could make a noticeable difference over a few weeks. When did you last have your levels checked?

like 4 months ago maybe? I keep meaning to book a blood test but finding time to go to the GP during busy season is basically impossible. I cant exactly tell my manager I need the morning off because I'm anemic init

Actually, you can — it's a medical appointment, and any decent employer should accommodate that. You don't even need to say what it's for, just "I have a medical appointment, I'll be in by 11." But if that feels awkward, a lot of GPs and clinics offer early morning or Saturday appointments. Some pharmacies even do blood tests now. Have you looked into that?

I havent but thats a good shout. theres a Boots near my office I think they do health checks. the thing is I KNOW my levels will be low because I can feel it. the tiredness is different from just being overworked. like theres a specific kind of exhaustion with anemia where your body just feels heavy and your brain is foggy. I know that feeling well

You're right that it's a distinct kind of fatigue — iron deficiency exhaustion is that deep, bone-level tiredness that sleep doesn't fix. And if you're layering that on top of 15-hour work days with 5 hours of sleep, it's no wonder you feel like you might pass out. Getting your levels checked would at least tell you if the ferrous sulfate dose needs adjusting. Your GP might also consider an iron infusion if oral supplements aren't keeping you at adequate levels.

an infusion would be dead useful actually. my cousin had one and she said the difference was like night and day. maybe I should push for that. I feel like I've been taking these tablets for years and just accepting that I'll always be tired and thats just my life

You shouldn't have to accept constant fatigue as your baseline. If oral supplements have kept you at the low end of normal for years despite consistent use, that's exactly the kind of situation where an infusion might be appropriate. When you do get in to see your GP, advocate for it — bring up the poor absorption, the fact that you've been on ferrous sulfate for years, and that your levels are still borderline. You know your body better than anyone.

yeah I'm going to book something in January when things are slightly less chaotic. or actually no I'll do it this week before I forget again. thanks for this, I needed someone to tell me to stop ignoring it. busy season plus anemia is genuinely the worst combination and I've just been powering through like an idiot

You're not an idiot — you're just someone with a lot on her plate who's been deprioritizing her own health. Book the appointment this week while you're thinking about it, and start separating the iron from the coffee tomorrow morning. Small steps. Hope you start feeling better soon.
